title: Cohort composition
desc: Class balance: diabetic vs non-diabetic patients.

title: Features per modality
desc: How many measurements each clinical modality contributes.

title: Data density by modality
desc: Fraction of non-zero entries per modality (sparsity of meds/dx vs labs/vitals).

title: item_220210 by status
desc: Overlaid distributions of the top predictor split by diabetes status (dashed = medians).

title: Top-6 feature boxplots
desc: Distribution of the six most discriminative continuous features, split by status.

title: Univariate discriminative power
desc: Each feature's standalone ability to separate diabetics (AUC), coloured by modality.

title: Volcano plot
desc: Effect size (Cohen's d) against statistical significance for every feature; labelled points are the strongest.

title: Stable-feature correlations
desc: Pearson correlations among the stably-selected features (redundancy check).

title: Odds ratios (logistic)
desc: Multivariable-adjusted odds ratios per 1-SD increase, 95% bootstrap CI; vertical line = no effect.

title: Per-fold performance
desc: AUC / accuracy / F1 for each outer fold (dotted = mean AUC); shows estimate stability.

title: ROC curve
desc: Out-of-fold ROC; area under curve summarises ranking quality.

title: Precision-Recall curve
desc: PR curve — more informative than ROC under class imbalance; AP = average precision.

title: Calibration curve
desc: Are predicted probabilities trustworthy? Points on the diagonal = well-calibrated.

title: Probability separation
desc: Distribution of predicted risk for each true class; overlap = where mistakes happen.

title: Threshold sweep
desc: How sensitivity/specificity/F1 trade off with the decision threshold; red = optimal Youden point.

title: Confusion matrix
desc: Out-of-fold confusion as raw counts and per-class rates.

title: Selection stability
desc: How often each feature is chosen across folds; past the line = stable/trustworthy.

title: Permutation importance
desc: Which stable features actually drive the model (AUC lost when each is scrambled).

title: Method comparison
desc: QSQ-FS vs 7 baselines on AUC/Accuracy/F1 under one fair protocol.

title: Accuracy vs parsimony
desc: AUC against feature count: top-left = accurate AND simple. QSQ-FS (star) uses ~half the features.
